page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1ne.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, this server provides 32 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core running at a base clock speed of 2.5 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25%, and single-threaded gzip compression registering in the bottom 10%. However, the 32 dedicated cores compensate in parallel workloads, delivering average multi-core performance across stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is average, though the Passmark memory score is weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.sn1ne.8xlarge offers a high density of dedicated physical cores on an older hardware architecture, making it suitable for multi-threaded workloads that do not demand high single-thread clock speeds. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment. This instance is best suited for parallel processing tasks, multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, and moderate database operations.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core execution speeds are weak, falling into the bottom 25%, whereas multi-core throughput is average, supported by the high core count. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for parallelized workloads rather than single-threaded tasks. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
